content.rendered:
The Finnish vocabulary on this page is roughly divided in three different levels. However, my own point of view is that as a foreign language learner you should pick the subjects you want to talk about rather than blindly following someone’s suggestion. Good luck to everyone trying to broaden their vocabulary!

Table of Contents
  1. Vocabulary lists
    1. For beginners
    2. For intermediate learners
    3. For advanced learners
  2. Semantics: confusing words
  3. Derivations: words ending in…
  4. Word types

VOCABULARY LISTS

Semantics

SEMANTICS: Confusing words

  • Similar Verbs
    • Osata saada voida pystyä kyetä
    • Katsoa nähdä katsella
    • Sanoa puhua kertoa
    • Alkaa aloittaa ruveta ryhtyä
    • Taitaa saattaa mahtaa
    • Yrittää pyrkiä sovittaa
    • Luulla ajatella miettiä
    • Pyytää kehottaa käskeä
    • Viedä tuoda hakea
  • Many Meanings
    • The verb saada
    • The verb kestää
    • The verb käydä
    • The verb katsoa
    • The verb antaa
  • Closely Related Verbs
    • Puhua and talking
    • Nukkua and sleeping
    • Syödä and eating
  • How to say
    • “Open” and “closed”
    • “Here” and “there”
    • “Broken” and “to break”

    • “Nothing nowhere never”
    • “Anything anywhere anytime”

    • “I can” in Finnish
    • “I cook” in Finnish
    • How to say “for”
    • How to say “again”

    • A LACK of something
  • How to use
    • Mones

    • Uudestaan uudelleen
  • The Difference Between
    • Sitten vs. silloin
    • Kun vs. kuin

    • Joku vs. jokin
    • Vaan vs. mutta
    • Moni monta monet
    • Paljon vs. monet
    • Elefantti vs. norsu

    • Lopussa loppuun lopuksi
  • Random words
    • Aika
    • Heinä
    • Kissa
Derivations

DERIVATIONS: Words ending in…

  • Words ending in
    • Työtön, avuton: –tOn
    • Vanhuus, terveys: –UUs
    • Opetus, ymmärrys: –Us
    • Kysymys, hakemus: –mUs
    • Avanto, luonto: –ntO
    • Kysyntä, uusinta: –ntA
    • Kirjasto, väestö: –stO
    • Sattuma, esitelmä: –mA
    • Myyjä, opiskelija: –jA
    • Ystävätär, laulajatar: –tAr
    • Soitin, keitin: –in
    • Ostos, päätös: -Os
    • Aavikko, kolikko: -kkO
    • Hylätty, tunnettu: -ttU
    • Myymälä, neuvola: -lA
  • Comparisons
    • Words ending in -s
  • Words beginning in –epä
  • Words ending in
    • Kallis, kaunis: –is
    • Roskis, futis: –is
    • Hammas, sairas: –As
    • Jälkkäri, makkari: –Ari
    • Väsynyt, uupunut: –nUt
    • Kyynel, manner: -l, -n, -r
    • Hame, nalle: -e
    • Plurale tantum words
    • Nouns ending in -nen
    • Common adjectives: -nen
    • Loanwords ending in -nen
    • Words ending in -inen
    • Words ending in -llinen
    • Words ending in -mAinen
  • Adverbs ending in
    • Adverbs ending in -sti
    • Adverbs ending in –ttain
    • Adverbs ending in -kkain
    • Adverbs ending in -isin
  • Verb derivations
    • Translative verbs
    • Transitivity overview
    • Automative verbs -UA
    • Automative verbs -UTUA
    • Reflexive verbs – UtUA
    • Causative verbs
    • Emotive causative verbs
    • Curative verbs
    • Verbs ending in -OITTAA
Word types

WORD TYPES

  • Pronouns
    • Overview
    • Demonstrative: tämä tuo se
    • Interrogative: question words
    • Relative pronoun joka
    • Joka jonka jota joiden
    • Joku and jokin
    • Reflexive pronoun itse
    • Pronoun toinen
  • Conjunctions
    • Conjunctions overview
    • Conjunctions and other connectives
    • Ja sekä ynnä
    • Paired conjunctions
    • Connectives (siinä tapauksessa)
    • Conjunction contractions
    • Muttei vaikkei ettei
  • Post- and Prepositions
    • Postpositions
    • The inflection of postpositions
    • Prepositions
    • Post- and prepositions list
  • Adjectives
    • Most common adjectives
    • Proadjectives tällainen sellainen
  • Adverbs
    • Adverbs overview
    • Adverbs of time
    • Adverbs of manner
    • Adverbs of intensity
    • Adverbs of location
    • Adverbs of probability
  • Word families: liike liikkua

excerpt.rendered: The Finnish vocabulary on this page is roughly divided in three different levels. However, my own point of view is that as a foreign language learner you should pick the subjects you want to talk about rather than blindly following someone’s suggestion. Good luck to everyone trying to broaden their vocabulary!
